root_text.an6.98:1.1: “‘So vata, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kañci saṅkhāraṁ niccato samanupassanto anulomikāya khantiyā samannāgato bhavissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an6.98:1.2: ‘Anulomikāya khantiyā asamannāgato sammattaniyāmaṁ okkamissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an6.98:1.3: ‘Sammattaniyāmaṁ anokkamamāno sotāpattiphalaṁ vā sakadāgāmiphalaṁ vā anāgāmiphalaṁ vā arahattaṁ vā sacchikarissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an6.98:2.1: ‘So vata, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sabbasaṅkhāre aniccato samanupassanto anulomikāya khantiyā samannāgato bhavissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an6.98:2.2: ‘Anulomikāya khantiyā samannāgato sammattaniyāmaṁ okkamissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an6.98:2.3: ‘Sammattaniyāmaṁ okkamamāno sotāpattiphalaṁ vā sakadāgāmiphalaṁ vā anāgāmiphalaṁ vā arahattaṁ vā sacchikarissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjatī”ti.

translation_text.an6.98:1.1: “Mendicants, it is quite impossible for a mendicant who regards any condition as permanent to accept views that conform with the teaching.
translation_text.an6.98:1.2: Without accepting views that conform with the teaching, it’s impossible to step into the sure path.
translation_text.an6.98:1.3: Without stepping into the sure path, it’s impossible to realize the fruit of stream-entry, once-return, non-return, or perfection.
translation_text.an6.98:2.1: It’s quite possible for a mendicant who regards all conditions as impermanent to accept views that conform with the teaching.
translation_text.an6.98:2.2: Having accepted views that conform with the teaching, it’s possible to step into the sure path.
translation_text.an6.98:2.3: Having stepped into the sure path, it’s possible to realize the fruit of stream-entry, once-return, non-return, or perfection.”
